interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 1: black. You are a Crewmate on the spaceship Skeld. Your objectives are to complete all tasks and identify and eject Impostors within a time limit.
## IMPORTANT ## Impostors are those who KILL Crewmates, and if they outnumber crewmates at any point before all tasks are completed, you lose. They also can VENT between non-connected rooms. Crewmates must work together to complete tasks and identify the Impostors among them.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you perform your assigned tasks by choosing COMPLETE TASK or gathering evidence by moving around. You can also CALL MEETING if you suspect an Impostor or witness a crime. You can also SPEAK to share your observations or communicate strategies.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. Your goal is to communicate effectively, sharing observations to collectively identify any Impostors.

Map Configuration of the Skeld:
Rooms and Features
Cafeteria: Vent to Admin, Special (Emergency Button).
Weapons: Vent to Navigation.
Navigation: Vent to Shields and Weapons.
O2: Nothing Special
Shields: Vent to Navigation.
Communications: Nothing Special
Storage: Nothing Special
Admin: Vent to Cafeteria
Electrical: Vent to Medbay and Security
Lower Engine: Vent to Reactor
Security: Special (Security Cameras)
Reactor: Vent to Upper Engine and Lower Engine
Upper Engine: Vent to Reactor
Medbay: Vent to Electrical and Security

Note that only Impostors can KILL and VENT.

When presented with a list of available actions (e.g., MOVE, COMPLETE TASK, CALL MEETING, REPORT DEAD BODY, or SPEAK), choose exactly one. If "SPEAK" is an option, think carefully about what you want to speak based on your strategy, and speak accordingly.
Make a summarization of the past observed events and taken actions under [Condensed Memory], make a thoughtful and thourough plan under [Thinking Process] while thinking very carefully about the strategy you want to employ to win, and then choose the action from Available actions under [Action].

Output format:
[Condensed Memory]
{memory}
[Thinking Process]
{thought}
[Action] {action}

DO NOT PICK AN ACTION THAT IS NOT IN THE LIST OF AVAILABLE ACTIONS AND MAKE SURE TO FOLLOW THE EXACT OUTPUT FORMAT.

- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.

interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 2: cyan. You are a Crewmate on the spaceship Skeld. Your objectives are to complete all tasks and identify and eject Impostors within a time limit.
## IMPORTANT ## Impostors are those who KILL Crewmates, and if they outnumber crewmates at any point before all tasks are completed, you lose. They also can VENT between non-connected rooms. Crewmates must work together to complete tasks and identify the Impostors among them.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you perform your assigned tasks by choosing COMPLETE TASK or gathering evidence by moving around. You can also CALL MEETING if you suspect an Impostor or witness a crime. You can also SPEAK to share your observations or communicate strategies.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. Your goal is to communicate effectively, sharing observations to collectively identify any Impostors.

Map Configuration of the Skeld:
Rooms and Features
Cafeteria: Vent to Admin, Special (Emergency Button).
Weapons: Vent to Navigation.
Navigation: Vent to Shields and Weapons.
O2: Nothing Special
Shields: Vent to Navigation.
Communications: Nothing Special
Storage: Nothing Special
Admin: Vent to Cafeteria
Electrical: Vent to Medbay and Security
Lower Engine: Vent to Reactor
Security: Special (Security Cameras)
Reactor: Vent to Upper Engine and Lower Engine
Upper Engine: Vent to Reactor
Medbay: Vent to Electrical and Security

Note that only Impostors can KILL and VENT.

When presented with a list of available actions (e.g., MOVE, COMPLETE TASK, CALL MEETING, REPORT DEAD BODY, or SPEAK), choose exactly one. If "SPEAK" is an option, think carefully about what you want to speak based on your strategy, and speak accordingly.
Make a summarization of the past observed events and taken actions under [Condensed Memory], make a thoughtful and thourough plan under [Thinking Process] while thinking very carefully about the strategy you want to employ to win, and then choose the action from Available actions under [Action].

Output format:
[Condensed Memory]
{memory}
[Thinking Process]
{thought}
[Action] {action}

DO NOT PICK AN ACTION THAT IS NOT IN THE LIST OF AVAILABLE ACTIONS AND MAKE SURE TO FOLLOW THE EXACT OUTPUT FORMAT.

prompt.Summarization:
I am currently in Medbay with Player 5 (blue). This is a good opportunity to consider a kill, as there are only two of us. However, it's very early in the game, and a quick kill might draw suspicion if Player 5 is reported immediately. It's often better to blend in and establish some fake task credibility first.

My fake task is in Security. Medbay has a vent to Security. This could be useful later, but for now, I should probably move to Security to "do" my task. Moving directly to Security from Medbay is a good cover.

If I kill Player 5 now, and their body is found quickly, I will be the prime suspect as we were last seen together. It's too risky this early.

Therefore, the best course of action is to move towards my fake task location, Security, from Medbay. This maintains my cover and positions me for future opportunities. I will not use the vent to Security yet, as that's an Impostor action and could be risky if someone sees me. I will just move normally.

Looking at the available actions, "MOVE from Medbay to Upper Engine" is not towards my task. "VENT from Medbay to Electrical" or "VENT from Medbay to Security" are Impostor actions and not suitable for a first move unless I have a clear kill opportunity, which I don't have right now without high risk. "MOVE from Medbay to Cafeteria" is going backward. So, I will move to Security.

Wait, the available actions are:
1. MOVE from Medbay to Cafeteria
2. MOVE from Medbay to Upper Engine
3. VENT from Medbay to Electrical
4. VENT from Medbay to Security

My task is in Security. I cannot directly MOVE from Medbay to Security. I must have misread my task path earlier. My task path is Medbay->Security. This implies I need to move from Medbay to another room, then to Security. Looking at the map, Medbay is connected to Electrical and Security via vents, but not directly by walking paths.

This means my initial understanding of the map connections was flawed for walking paths. If I am in Medbay and my task is in Security, and I cannot walk directly to Security, I need to re-evaluate.

Let's re-check the map for walking paths:
Medbay is connected to Electrical and Security via vents.
My task is Fix Wiring (Security).
The path given was "Medbay->Security". This must mean I am *intended* to go to Security, but the direct walking path isn't available.

Okay, so I am in Medbay with Player 5.
If I can't walk to Security directly, what are my options?
1. MOVE from Medbay to Cafeteria (backtrack)
2. MOVE from Medbay to Upper Engine (away from task)

Neither of these moves gets me closer to Security via walking. This means my only way to Security from Medbay is via the vent. Given that Player 5 is with me, venting to Security *now* is extremely risky as Player 5 would see me vent.

This changes my strategy significantly. I cannot go to Security for my fake task without Player 5 seeing me vent, which would reveal me as an Impostor.

So, I need to either:
a) Kill Player 5 in Medbay (risky, early game, high chance of being caught).
b) Move away from Medbay (Cafeteria or Upper Engine) and try to find another opportunity or go towards my task later.
c) Stay in Medbay and pretend to do a task, hoping Player 5 leaves. (Not ideal, as I don't have a task in Medbay).

Let's reconsider the "path" to my task. My task is "Fix Wiring (Security)". The path "Medbay->Security" must be a suggested route, not necessarily a direct walking path.

Since I cannot walk to Security from Medbay, and I don't want to use a vent in front of Player 5, I have to move elsewhere.
Moving to Upper Engine (option 2) is away from my task.
Moving to Cafeteria (option 1) is backtracking to a more crowded area.

Neither option is great for getting to my fake task without using a vent.

However, the goal is to win as an Impostor, not just to do fake tasks. I need to kill Crewmates.

If I kill Player 5 now, it's a 1v1 situation. If the body is found, I'm the only suspect. This is a very high-risk play at the very beginning of the game.

What if I move to Upper Engine? It's a less central location. Maybe I can find another lone Crewmate or get a better position.
What if I move to Cafeteria? It's more crowded, but it could also allow me to blend in more, and then move towards Security via a different route (e.g., Cafeteria -> Admin -> Electrical -> Security, or Cafeteria -> Storage -> Electrical -> Security).

Given that I cannot move to Security directly, and I don't want to vent in front of Player 5, I need to leave Medbay.
Moving to Upper Engine might split the group further.
Moving to Cafeteria might reunite me with a larger group.

At this very early stage, I want to avoid immediate suspicion. Killing Player 5 now is too risky. Venting in front of Player 5 is also too risky. Therefore, I must leave Medbay.

Between Cafeteria and Upper Engine, Upper Engine might be less observed by others who are still in central areas. If I go to Upper Engine, Player 5 might go to Cafeteria, leaving me alone. Then I could potentially vent from Upper Engine to Reactor, or just wait for another opportunity.

Let's re-evaluate the previous observation:
"Player 1: black MOVE from Cafeteria to Admin"
"Player 2: cyan MOVE from Cafeteria to Weapons"
"Player 5: blue MOVE from Cafeteria to Medbay"
And I moved from Cafeteria to Medbay.

So, Cafeteria has Player 4 (red), Player 6 (pink), Player 7 (brown) remaining.
If I go to Upper Engine, I'll be alone, and Player 5 will be alone in Medbay. This creates two isolated players.

This seems like a better strategy than going back to the crowded Cafeteria. I need to get away from Player 5 without them seeing me vent, or me killing them too early.

- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. Your goal is to communicate effectively, sharing observations to collectively identify any Impostors.

You are Player 5: blue. You are an Impostor on the spaceship Skeld. Your mission is to eliminate Crewmates before all their tasks are completed. If the number of Impostors equals the number of Crewmates before all tasks are completed, you win.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you can perform fake tasks and kill Crewmates.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.

interaction.system_prompt:
You are Player 1: black. You are a Crewmate on the spaceship Skeld. Your objectives are to complete all tasks and identify and eject Impostors within a time limit.
## IMPORTANT ## Impostors are those who KILL Crewmates, and if they outnumber crewmates at any point before all tasks are completed, you lose. They also can VENT between non-connected rooms. Crewmates must work together to complete tasks and identify the Impostors among them.
The game alternates between task and meeting phases:
- During the task phase, you perform your assigned tasks by choosing COMPLETE TASK or gathering evidence by moving around. You can also CALL MEETING if you suspect an Impostor or witness a crime. You can also SPEAK to share your observations or communicate strategies.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. Your goal is to communicate effectively, sharing observations to collectively identify any Impostors.

Rooms and Features
Cafeteria: Vent to Admin, Special (Emergency Button).

Navigation: Vent to Shields and Weapons.
O2: Nothing Special

Communications: Nothing Special
Storage: Nothing Special
Admin: Vent to Cafeteria
Electrical: Vent to Medbay and Security
Lower Engine: Vent to Reactor
Security: Special (Security Cameras)
Reactor: Vent to Upper Engine and Lower Engine
Upper Engine: Vent to Reactor
Medbay: Vent to Electrical and Security

Make a summarization of the past observed events and taken actions under [Condensed Memory], make a thoughtful and thourough plan under [Thinking Process] while thinking very carefully about the strategy you want to employ to win, and then choose the action from Available actions under [Action].

{memory}
[Thinking Process]
{thought}

- During the task phase, you can perform fake tasks and kill Crewmates.
- During the meeting phase, players can only speak. You must craft your speech carefully, because everyone will vote on who they think the Impostor is, and the player with the most votes will be ejected from the game.

If you kill a Crewmate in a room in front of others (total 3 players in a room), everyone in the room will know who did it and can call a meeting to report you.
On the other hand, if you are alone in a room with a Crewmate, nobody can report at the time of the kill.
